LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION (CCP 583.410). ARGUED AND THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING ISSUED FOR THE ORIGINAL HEARING DATE OF MAY 10, 2012. DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING RENEWED ON OR AFTER DECEMBER 1, 2012 IF THE CASE IS NOT RESOLVED BEFORE THEN AND THE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT TAKEN SUBSTANTIAL STEPS TOWARD GETTING THE CASE RESOLVED. PLAINTIFF'S MINIMAL DILIGENCE, COUPLED WITH THE STRONG POLICY IN FAVOR OF RESOLVING CASES ON THEIR MERITS, FAVORS DENIAL OF THE MOTION AT THIS POINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER TO THE COURT BY EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

Notice Of Motion And Otion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution (Ccp 583.410); Memo Of P&A; Declaration
Off Calendar May-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION (CCP 583.410).
Appearances: Roni Rotholz (925.932.0193) for plaintiff and responding party; Gregory B. Mouroux of AT&T Services Legal Department (778.1359) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court ruled as follows: the court adopts its tentative ruling issued for the original hearing date of May 10, 2012. Denied without prejudice to being renewed on or after December 1, 2012 if the case is not resolved before then and the plaintiff has not taken substantial steps toward getting the case resolved. Plaintiff's minimal diligence, coupled with the strong policy in favor of resolving cases on their merits, favors denial of the motion at this point. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order to the court by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Notice Of Motion And Otion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution (Ccp 583.410); Memo Of P&A; Declaration
Off Calendar May-10-2012 Continued To May-17-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PACIFIC BELL TELEPHONE COMPANY'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION.
Appearances: Gregory B. Mouroux of AT&T Services Legal Department (778.1359) for defendant and moving party; Roni Rotholz appearing telephonically with assistant Jean Woltz for plaintiff and responding party.
This matter was re-called due to no appearance for plaintiff. Mr. Rotholz called into the courtroom mid-morning. Discussed and the court continued the matter to May 17, 2012 for argument. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153

Ntc Of Motion To Allow Late Filing
Off Calendar May-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PENNY LEVY MOTION TO ALLOW LATE FILING.
Appearances: Gregory B. Mouroux of AT&T Services Legal Department (778.1359) for defendant and moving party; Roni Rotholz appearing telephonically with assistant Jean Woltz for plaintiff and responding party.
This matter was not argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
Granted. The Court will consider Plaintiff's late-filed opposition in the interests of justice to avoid prejudicing the plaintiff for her counsel's inaction. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153

Notice And Motion To Waive Ordered Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Penny Levi's Motion to Waive Ordered Sanctions issued to Roni Rotholz, Esq., on 10/5/2009 in the amount of $350.00 and on 11/22/2010 in the amount of $350.00, is scheduled this day. There being no appearance nor an objection tentative ruling to grant motion is adopted. Motion is ordered granted. Sanctions are ordered vacated.
